title: Section 44

source.title: NPNF2-03. Theodoret, Jerome, Gennadius, & Rufinus: Historical Writings

verses.book: A Commentary on the Apostles' Creed.
verses.text: But that you may not suppose this to be a novel doctrine peculiar to Paul, I will adduce also what the Prophet Ezekiel foretold by the Holy Ghost.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Apostles' Creed.
verses.text: “Behold,” saith he, “I will open your graves and bring you forth out of your graves.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Apostles' Creed.
verses.text: Let me recall, further, how Job, who abounds in mystical language, plainly predicts the resurrection of the dead.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Apostles' Creed.
verses.text: “There is hope for a tree; for if it be cut down it will sprout again, and its shoot shall never fail.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Apostles' Creed.
verses.text: But if its root have waxed old in the earth, and the stock thereof be dead in the dust, yet through the scent of water it will flourish again, and put forth shoots as a young plant.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Apostles' Creed.
verses.text: But man, if he be dead, is he departed and gone?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Apostles' Creed.
verses.text: And mortal man, if he have fallen, shall he be no more?”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Apostles' Creed.
verses.text: Dost thou not see, that in these words he is appealing to men’s sense of shame, as it were, and saying, “Is mankind so foolish, that when they see the stock of a tree which has been cut down shooting forth again from the ground, and dead wood again restored to life, they imagine their own case to have no likeness to that of wood or trees?”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Apostles' Creed.
verses.text: But to convince you that Job’s words are to be read as a question, when he says, “But mortal man when he hath fallen shall he not rise again?” take this proof from what follows; for he adds immediately, “But if a man be dead, shall he live?”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Apostles' Creed.
verses.text: And presently afterwards he says, “I will wait till I be made again;” and afterwards he repeats the same: “Who shall raise again upon the earth my skin, which is now draining this cup of suffering?”
